free_text: Polarized proton beam  at En = 150.5 MeV
consisting of a 1000 microm solicon surface barrier
detector followed by a sodium iodide scintillator. A
150 microm silicon surface barrier dE-detector and a
5 mm Si(Li) E-detector formed another telescope for
Besides the 2.5 mg/cm2 thick 9Be target, a 3.1 mg/cm2
CH2 target, a 3.32 mg/cm2 CD2 target, and a blank
target were mounted on a target ladder.
The angular separation between the two detectors was
calibrated by using a coincident measurement of p-d
of the quasifree peak (zero recoil momentum) as a
function of the two-body p-alpha c.m. scattering angle
SD:Digitized data from figs.3-10 of PR/C,31,1662
